    DTDFVocab:inputTo MDEFramework, UnrealEngine5
]

]

]

    base:desc "Message delivery for real-time data synchronization"

]

]

]

]

    DTDFVocab:enables InteractiveVisualizationService, SimulationTestingService
]

]

]

]

    base:desc "Maintenance scheduling insights from predictive analytics"
]

    base:desc "Scenario simulation results for operational testing"
]

instance ReportInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Automated reports for decision-making support"
]

    base:desc "Control lighting parameters (on/off state, brightness, color temperature in kelvin)"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control signals are transmitted from the digital twin to Building Control Systems (BCS) for operational adjustments, as described in Document 4. Specific command types, message formats, validation mechanisms, feedback systems, conflict resolution, and execution confirmation details are not provided in the documents."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "Real-time data exchange capabilities ensure continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT Framework serves as a central hub coordinating Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, and DT Engineers to facilitate bidirectional data exchange and holistic building life cycle management through a modular architecture with standardized extension points for service integration."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin lifecycle encompasses design-time modeling in BIM (Building Control Engineers define sensors, actuators, and controllers), automated deployment via Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) to generate deployable artifacts with minimal manual intervention (R2), and runtime operations involving real-time data synchronization using Eclipse Hono and Ditto (R3), interactive visualization for monitoring/control by Facility Managers, and extensibility through custom services via modular architecture (R4). Stakeholder roles govern each phase: Building Control Engineers handle initial modeling, Facility Managers operate physical infrastructure via the DT, and DT Engineers extend functionality."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is validated using the Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric, comprising timeliness (0.997), reliability (1.0), and availability (1.0), measured via a 1-second heartbeat mechanism over 12 minutes. Data integrity is ensured through Eclipse Ditto’s policy-based validation for sensor value ranges and units, and pre-execution checks for actuator commands. Fault tolerance mechanisms include Eclipse Hono’s at-least-once delivery and Ditto’s persistent event storage, maintaining synchronization and operational safety. Bidirectional communication is verified by confirming state updates in both directions between physical and digital twins."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono and Ditto facilitate communication between physical and digital components for real-time data exchange, but specific network protocols, topology, bandwidth requirements, latency specifications, redundancy mechanisms, and security protocols are not detailed in the documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "A modular architecture with standardized extension points supports scalable, service-oriented deployment and runtime configuration management for digital twins, enabling adjustments without full redeployment. Specific hosting platforms, computational requirements, storage specifications, and deployment models are not detailed in the documents."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Interoperability across heterogeneous systems via standardized data schemas and bidirectional communication protocols, with BIM integration enabling unified semantic foundation and automated artifact generation through model-driven engineering principles."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to Building Information Modeling (BIM) standards as the AECO industry standard. Real-time data integration employs Eclipse Ditto and Hono, which comply with IoT communication protocols like MQTT. Modular architecture includes standardized extension points for interoperability, addressing fragmented deployments by ensuring semantic integration and consistent data schemas."
]
